revision.comment: 新しいページ：上山2号墳（奈良県平群町にあった古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 上山2号墳 |画像 = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
移築復元[[横口式石槨]]（[[花園大学]]構内） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]下垣内 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 28.07 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 一辺10m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]] |出土品 = [[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 上山2号墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上山2号墳'''（うえやまにごうふん）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]下垣内にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 == 奈良県西部、[[竜田川]]西岸の廿日山丘陵の丘陵最高所南（現在の光ヶ丘住宅地南側）の緩斜面に築造された古墳である。北東の丘陵最高所には[[前方後円墳]]の上山1号墳がかつて所在し、丘陵南裾には西宮1号墳（[[西宮古墳]]）・西宮2号墳が所在する。[[1989年|1989]]-[[1990年]]（[[平成]]元-2年）の廿日山丘陵の宅地造成に伴う発掘調査の際に発見され、調査実施のうえで消滅している。 墳形は台形状の方形で、南辺10メートル・北辺7メートル・奥行9メートルを測った。墳丘周囲の東・北・西側には、幅1.0-1.5メートル・深さ0.5メートルの周溝がコ字形に巡らされる{{Sfn|村社仁史|1990}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南南東方向に開口した。中世墓として再利用されたため大きく破壊されており、調査では副葬品として[[須恵器]]片のみが検出されている。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|村社仁史|1990}}。廿日山丘陵には、古墳時代後期から終末期の方墳が分布するが、そのなかでも本古墳は平群谷で唯一となる横口式石槨を埋葬施設とする点で注目される。 現在では、石槨は[[花園大学]]構内に移築復元保存されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|復元石槨パース図}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|復元石槨展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南南南方向に開口した。石槨の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|村社仁史|1990}}。 * 石槨：長さ約2メートル、幅1メートル、高さ0.7メートル * 羨道：長さ3メートル、幅1メートル 中世に火葬墓として再利用されたため、調査時点で羨道部を中心に石槨の大部分が破壊されている。石槨の石材は[[花崗岩]]系で、内面・組合部分は平坦に加工される。床面には厚さ0.2メートルの板石を敷く。石槨内からは、中世墓撹乱層から鉄釘数本が（一部は2号墳の木棺釘か）、羨道部・前庭部から[[須恵器]]坏片が検出されている{{Sfn|村社仁史|1990}}。 Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|復元石槨俯瞰図 == 関連施設 == * [[花園大学]]（京都府京都市京区西ノ京壺ノ内町） - 構内中庭に上山2号墳の移築石槨を保存。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Wikicite|reference=ふるさとへぐり再発見70「上山二号墳」|ref=}}。 * {{Cite journal|和書|author=村社仁史|title=大和における飛鳥時代古墳の一例 -上山二号墳の発掘調査から-|date=1990-11|publisher=[[花園大学]]史学会|journal=花園史学|volume=|number=11|naid=|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|村社仁史|1990}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * [[西宮古墳]] * [[花園大学]] {{デフォルトソート:うえやまにこうふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]]

revision.text: * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]** {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:上山2号墳]][[ja:上山2号墳]] 2025

{{Japanese Kofun |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]** {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:上山2号墳]][[ja:上山2号墳]] 2025

{{Japanese Kofun |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]

Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]** {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] 2025

{{Japanese Kofun |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] 2025

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] 2025

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]

Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] 2025

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] 2025

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] 2025

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] to [[Category:qqqq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横口式石槨]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横口式石槨]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - revision.comment: Adding [[Category:方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:方墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - revision.comment: Adding [[Category:方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hōfun]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |latitude = 34|latitude_min = 37|latitude_sec = 28.07 |longitude = 135|longitude_min = 42|longitude_sec = 4.60 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |dimensions = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |attached_tombs = |construction_period = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Tumulus no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |icon = Hōfun |label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoguchi-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue ware bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]]
