revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |other_designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |other_designation = None |tangible_cultural_property = |notable_features = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == Burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] revision.comment: more fixes
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial_structure = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |excavated_items = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |other_designation = None |tangible_notes = |notable_features = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |other_designation = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "burial Facility" to "burial"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried_individual = |mausoleum = |other_designation = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |other_designation = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |other_designation = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} * revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川|12=draft}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳|12=draft}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[en:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] [[sn:amiyama No. 2 Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川|12=draft}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳|12=draft}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[en:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] [[sn:amiyama No. 2 Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun}}

Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[en:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] [[sn:amiyama No. 2 Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun}}

Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|ja|平群町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|ja|生駒郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsutagawa River|ja|竜田川|12=draft|12=draft}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Cave Tomb-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:上山2号墳]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[en:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] [[sn:amiyama No. 2 Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun}}

The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|ja|花園大学|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Ueyama No. 2 Kofun}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) -
