revision.text:
[[ja:上山2号墳]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] [[ja:上山2号墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsuta River|en|Tatsuta River|qid=Q11598373|lt=Tatsutagawa River|1=Tatsuta River}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|qq=}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|上山2号墳|version=97980799|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上山2号墳]] {{wikidata link|Q130455347}} [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |image = [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, sekkaku.jpg|280px]]
Relocated and restored {{ill|Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}}) |alt_name = |group = |location = Shimogaito, {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 37|coordinates = 28.07 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 42|coordinates = 4.60 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 10 m on each side |burial = {{ill|Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} |attached_tombs = |built = Mid-{{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |tangible_notes = |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Ueyama No. 2 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Ueyama No. 2 Kofun''' (上山2号墳) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The tumulus, shaped as a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, no longer exists. == Overview == The kofun was situated on the southern slope of the highest point of the Hazukayama Hill in western Nara Prefecture, near the west bank of the {{ill|Tatsuta River|en|Tatsuta River|qid=Q11598373|lt=Tatsutagawa River|1=Tatsuta River}}, just south of Hikari-ga-oka residential area. Nearby, the northeastern hilltop housed Ueyama No. 1 Kofun (a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}), while the southern base featured the Nishinomiya Nos. 1 and 2 Kofun (including {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}}). The site was discovered during residential development in 1989–1990, leading to its excavation and subsequent destruction. The tumulus measured 10 m on its southern side, 7 m on its northern side, and had a depth of 9 m. A U-shaped surrounding ditch, measuring 1.0–1.5 m wide and 0.5 m deep, was identified along the east, north, and west sides. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} The burial structure consisted of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. The structure was heavily damaged due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, with only fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed. The kofun is dated to the mid-{{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, in the terminal period of kofun construction. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Among the Hazukayama Hill tombs, this is the only one in the Heguri Valley with a yokoguchi-style stone chamber, making it particularly notable. Today, the stone chamber is preserved in a relocated and restored state on the campus of {{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}} in Kyoto. == burial Structure == [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Perspective View}}]] [[File:Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Restored Stone Chamber Diagram}}]] The burial structure featured a {{ill|Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-shiki sekaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} * Chamber: Approximately 2 m long, 1 m wide, 0.7 m high * Passageway: 3 m long, 1 m wide Due to its reuse as a medieval cremation grave, much of the chamber was destroyed, particularly the passageway. The chamber stones, made of granite, were flatly cut and fitted together. A 0.2 m thick stone slab covered the floor. Iron nails, some possibly from the original wooden coffin, were found in the disturbed medieval burial layer, alongside fragments of Sue pottery bowls recovered from the passage and forecourt. {{Sfn|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}} Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of restored stone chamber == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}} (Nishinokyo Tsubonouchi-chō, Kyoto): The restored stone chamber of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun is preserved in the campus courtyard. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Wikicite|reference=Furusato Heguri Rediscovery No. 70: "Ueyama No. 2 Kofun"|ref=}}. * {{Cite journal|author=Murasoro Hitoshi|title=A Case Study of Asuka Period Tombs in Yamato: Excavation of Ueyama No. 2 Kofun|date=1990-11|publisher={{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}} Historical Society|journal=Hanazono Shigaku|number=11|pages=75-83|ref={{Harvid|Murasoro Hitoshi|1990}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Ueyama Kofun Group|上山古墳群}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hanazono University|en|Hanazono University|qid=Q5646966|lt=Hanazono University|1=Hanazono University}} * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|ja|上山2号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama No. 2 Kofun|qq=}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|上山2号墳|version=97980799|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q130455347|ja|上山2号墳}} [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
